description:
This conceptual spatial gridding diagram illustrates the distribution of measurement points (MP) from two different Interferometric Synthetic Aperture Radar (InSAR) acquisition geometries, "Ascending" and "Descending", within a uniform grid system. Each acquisition geometry is represented by a 2x2 grid, conceptually part of a larger 100 m grid used in the European Ground Motion Service (EGMS) Ortho product.
For the "Ascending" geometry, represented by blue circular points:
- The top-left grid cell contains 11 points (green background).
- The top-right grid cell contains 1 point (peach background).
- The bottom-left grid cell contains 1 point (peach background).
- The bottom-right grid cell contains 6 points (green background).
For the "Descending" geometry, represented by orange circular points:
- The top-left grid cell contains 10 points (green background).
- The top-right grid cell contains 3 points (peach background).
- The bottom-left grid cell contains 9 points (green background).
- The bottom-right grid cell contains 8 points (green background).
The green background colour in cells indicates a higher density of measurement points, while the peach background indicates a lower density of points, demonstrating how raw InSAR data from different passes are spatially sampled and aggregated into grid cells for further processing.
